claim: Context engineering is a discipline of systematically providing models with relevant information, tools, and instructions in correct format and time; shifts focus from prompt wording to comprehensive context design
actionable: Stop optimizing prompts in isolation; instead design complete context systems that deliver information at the right time in the right format for agent reasoning
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Nyah Macklin: How to Structure Context to Make Your Agents Smarter

claim: Pure vector RAG retrieves semantically similar fragments but misses multi-hop reasoning; hybrid RAG (vector + keyword + metadata + reranking) ensures relevance and prevents context poisoning
actionable: Use hybrid RAG for agents: combine vector search with keyword/metadata filters and rerank results; this prevents irrelevant context from poisoning agent reasoning
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Nyah Macklin: How to Structure Context to Make Your Agents Smarter

claim: Knowledge graphs encode facts within relationships, enabling multi-hop reasoning that vector search misses; agents can traverse edges to find connected context in a single reasoning loop
actionable: Convert tabular/unstructured data into knowledge graphs for agent retrieval; agents can reason across connected entities that vector similarity would miss
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Nyah Macklin: How to Structure Context to Make Your Agents Smarter

claim: Graph RAG reduced per-issue customer service resolution time by 28.6% compared to traditional RAG; LinkedIn reduced ticket resolution from 40 to 15 hours using graph context
actionable: Implement knowledge graphs for customer support agents to reduce resolution time significantly; the structured reasoning from graphs beats pure semantic search
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Nyah Macklin: How to Structure Context to Make Your Agents Smarter

claim: Graph-based context enables complex multi-hop queries (e.g., 'which asset managers vulnerable to lithium shortage') by traversing entity connections; vector RAG gives generic answers
actionable: For multi-hop analytical queries, structure domain knowledge as graphs and query via graph traversal; this gives specific answers rather than generic model responses
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Nyah Macklin: How to Structure Context to Make Your Agents Smarter

claim: Memory management trade-off: sliding window drops older history but loses important details; summarization and episodic memory preserve context but require careful design to avoid overload
actionable: Design agent memory carefully: use sliding window for short sessions, add summarization for long conversations, preserve episodic boundaries to avoid context explosion
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Nyah Macklin: How to Structure Context to Make Your Agents Smarter

claim: Placing most relevant information at top of prompt improves model focus; consistent delimiters/labels for tool outputs and templates for memory improve reasoning reliability
actionable: Organize agent context with most critical info first; use consistent formatting (delimiters, labels) for different context types to guide model attention
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Nyah Macklin: How to Structure Context to Make Your Agents Smarter

claim: Offload complex calculations and data retrieval to tools rather than stuffing context with raw data; agents stay focused on reasoning while tools handle heavy lifting
actionable: Design agents to use database query tools instead of embedding large tables in context; this preserves tokens for reasoning while enabling data access
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Nyah Macklin: How to Structure Context to Make Your Agents Smarter
